verified_examples.name: Node_Nav_1
verified_examples.code:
# Node Navigation 1
parent = hou.node('/obj/python_tester')
geo = parent.createNode('geo', 'mygeo_0')
box = geo.createNode('box', 'box_0')
xform = geo.createNode('xform', 'xform_0')
xform.setInput(0, box)
xform.moveToGoodPosition()
null = geo.createNode('null', 'OUT_0')
null.setInput(0, xform)
null.moveToGoodPosition()
null.setDisplayFlag(True)
null.setRenderFlag(True)
verified_examples.explanation: In this Python HOM example (Node_Nav_1), the script creates new nodes in the Houdini network and connects nodes together.

verified_examples.name: Params_Anim_1
verified_examples.code:
# Parameters 1
parent = hou.node('/obj/python_tester')
geo = parent.createNode('geo')
sphere = geo.createNode('sphere')
sphere.parm('type').set(1) # Polygon
sphere.parm('radx').set(1.05)
sphere.parm('rady').set(1.05)
key = hou.Keyframe()
key.setFrame(1)
key.setValue(0)
sphere.parm('tx').setKeyframe(key)
key.setFrame(24)
key.setValue(1.05)
sphere.parm('tx').setKeyframe(key)
sphere.parm('ty').setExpression('$F / 24.0')
verified_examples.explanation: In this Python HOM example (Params_Anim_1), the script creates new nodes in the Houdini network, sets node parameter values, creates and sets animation keyframes and sets an HScript expression on a parameter.

verified_examples.name: HOM_Geometry_1
verified_examples.code:
# HOM Geometry 1
parent = hou.node('/obj/python_tester')
geo_node = parent.createNode('geo')
box = geo_node.createNode('box')
python = geo_node.createNode('python')
python.setInput(0, box)
python_code = '''node = hou.pwd()\ngeo = node.geometry()\ncd = geo.addAttrib(hou.attribType.Point, 'Cd', (1.0, 1.0, 1.0))\nfor pt in geo.points():\n    pos = pt.position()\n    pt.setPosition(pos * 4.78)\n    pt.setAttribValue(cd, (pos[0], pos[1], pos[2]))'''
python.parm('python').set(python_code)
python.cook(force=True)
verified_examples.explanation: In this Python HOM example (HOM_Geometry_1), the script creates new nodes in the Houdini network, connects nodes together, sets node parameter values, accesses the node's underlying geometry, creates a new geometry attribute and forces the node to cook and evaluate.

verified_examples.name: HOM_Geometry_2
verified_examples.code:
# HOM Geometry 2
parent = hou.node('/obj/python_tester')

python = geo_node.createNode('python')
python.setInput(0, box)
python_code = '''node = hou.pwd()\ngeo = node.geometry()\ncd = geo.addAttrib(hou.attribType.Point, 'Cd', (1.0, 1.0, 1.0))\nfor pt in geo.points():\n    pos = pt.position()\n    pt.setPosition(pos * 9.25)\n    pt.setAttribValue(cd, (pos[0], pos[1], pos[2]))'''

verified_examples.name: Attr_Groups_1
verified_examples.code:
# Attributes & Groups 1
parent = hou.node('/obj/python_tester')
geo = parent.createNode('geo')
box = geo.createNode('box')
grp = geo.createNode('groupcreate')
grp.setInput(0, box)
grp.parm('groupname').set('my_group_0')
grp.parm('groupbase').set(0)
grp.parm('groupbounding').set(1)
grp.parm('sizex').set(1.5)
grp.cook(force=True)
geom = grp.geometry()
points = geom.points()
point_group = geom.findPointGroup('my_group_0')
if point_group:
    pts_in_group = point_group.points()
verified_examples.explanation: In this Python HOM example (Attr_Groups_1), the script creates new nodes in the Houdini network, connects nodes together, sets node parameter values, accesses the node's underlying geometry, queries geometry point groups and forces the node to cook and evaluate.

verified_examples.name: Math_VecMat_Fix_1
verified_examples.code:
# Math HOM 1
v1 = hou.Vector3(-2.71, 1, 0)
v2 = hou.Vector3(0, -2.71, 1)
dist = v1.distanceTo(v2)
dot = v1.dot(v2)
cross = v1.cross(v2)
norm = v1.normalized()
m_trans = hou.hmath.buildTranslate(v1)
m_rot = hou.hmath.buildRotate(45, 0, 0)
m_scale = hou.hmath.buildScale(2, 2, 2)
m4 = m_scale * m_rot * m_trans
q = hou.Quaternion(m_rot)
v3 = v2 * m4
verified_examples.explanation: In this Python HOM example (Math_VecMat_Fix_1), the script uses HOM math classes for vector/matrix calculations.
